Let me analyze this issue. The problem is that ktlint is wrapping a block comment inside a single-line block, turning code like:

```kotlin
val foo = { /* no-op */ }
```

into something like:
```kotlin
val foo = { 
  /* no-op */ 
}
```

Based on the relevant files listed, the most likely culprits are:
- `CommentWrappingRule.kt` - since it deals with comment wrapping
- `WrappingRule.kt` - general wrapping rule
- Other wrapping rules that might affect blocks

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT

  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.hasNewLineInClosedRange
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 15→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 18→
 19→/**
 20→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 21→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22→ */
 23→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24→    StandardRule(
 25→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 27→            setOf(
 28→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 29→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 30→            ),
 31→    ) {
 32→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 33→        node: ASTNode,
 34→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 35→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 36→    ) {
 37→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39→                node
 40→                    .prevLeaf()
 41→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43→                node
 44→                    .nextLeaf()
 45→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46→
 47→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49→            ) {
 50→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53→                    //    some comment
 54→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55→                    emit(
 56→                        node.startOffset,
 57→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58→                            "disallowed",
 59→                        false,
 60→                    )
 61→                } else {
 62→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64→                    emit(
 65→                        node.startOffset,
 66→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 67→                        false,
 68→                    )
 69→                }
 70→                return
 71→            }
 72→
 73→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 74→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 75→
 76→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 77→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 78→        }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 82→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 85→    ) {
 86→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 87→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 88→            emit(
 89→                startOffset,
 90→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
 91→                true,
 92→            )
 93→            if (autoCorrect) {
 94→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
 95→            }
 96→        } else {
 97→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
 98→            emit(
 99→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
100→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
101→                false,
102→            )
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
107→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110→        rawRemove()
111→    }
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
114→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116→        autoCorrect: Boolean,

 21→/**
 22→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 23→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.

 25→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 26→    StandardRule(
 27→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 28→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 29→            setOf(
 30→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 31→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,

 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .prevLeaf()
 43→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 44→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 45→                node
 46→                    .nextLeaf()
 47→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 48→
 49→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 50→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 51→            ) {
 52→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 53→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 54→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 55→                    //    some comment
 56→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 57→                    emit(
 58→                        node.startOffset,
 59→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 60→                            "disallowed",
 61→                        false,
 62→                    )
 63→                } else if (isSingleLineBlockWithOnlyComment(
 64→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment,
 65→                        node,
 66→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment,
 67→                    )
 68→                ) {
 69→                    // Allow single line blocks containing only a comment, like: { /* no-op */ }
 70→                    return
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 73→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 74→                    emit(
 75→                        node.startOffset,
 76→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 77→                        false,
 78→                    )
 79→                }
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→
 83→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 84→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85→
 86→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 87→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→        }

 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 97→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                startOffset,
100→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
105→            }
106→        } else {
107→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
108→            emit(
109→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,

111→                false,
112→            )
113→        }
114→    }

117→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()

119→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)

126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
131→        }
132→    }

 21→/**
 22→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 23→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 24→ */
 25→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 26→    StandardRule(
 27→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 28→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 29→            setOf(
 30→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 31→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 32→            ),
 33→    ) {
 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .prevLeaf()
 43→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 44→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 45→                node
 46→                    .nextLeaf()
 47→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 48→
 49→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 50→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 51→            ) {
 52→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 53→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 54→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 55→                    //    some comment
 56→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 57→                    emit(
 58→                        node.startOffset,
 59→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 60→                            "disallowed",
 61→                        false,
 62→                    )
 63→                } else if (isSingleLineBlockWithOnlyComment(
 64→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment,
 65→                        node,
 66→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment,
 67→                    )
 68→                ) {
 69→                    // Allow single line blocks containing only a comment, like: { /* no-op */ }
 70→                    return
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 73→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 74→                    emit(
 75→                        node.startOffset,
 76→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 77→                        false,
 78→                    )
 79→                }
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→
 83→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 84→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85→
 86→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 87→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 92→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 97→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                startOffset,
100→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
105→            }
106→        } else {
107→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
108→            emit(
109→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
110→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
111→                false,
112→            )
113→        }
114→    }
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
117→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
118→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
119→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
120→        rawRemove()
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
124→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
135→
136→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
137→
138→    private fun isSingleLineBlockWithOnlyComment(
139→        precedingLeaf: ASTNode,
140→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
141→        followingLeaf: ASTNode,
142→    ): Boolean {
143→        // Check if this is a single-line block containing only a comment: { /* comment */ }
144→        // Need to find the actual non-whitespace tokens before and after
145→        val actualPrecedingLeaf = if (precedingLeaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
146→            precedingLeaf.prevLeaf()
147→        } else {
148→            precedingLeaf
149→        }
150→        val actualFollowingLeaf = if (followingLeaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE) {
151→            followingLeaf.nextLeaf()
152→        } else {
153→            followingLeaf
154→        }
155→
156→        return actualPrecedingLeaf?.elementType == LBRACE &&
157→            actualFollowingLeaf?.elementType == RBRACE &&
158→            !blockCommentNode.textContains('\n')
159→    }
160→}

Perfect! Let me create a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

I've successfully fixed the issue where ktlint was incorrectly reporting violations for single-line blocks containing only block comments, such as `val foo = { /* no-op */ }`.

### Changes Made

1. **Modified `CommentWrappingRule.kt`**:
   - Added imports for `LBRACE` and `RBRACE` element types (lines 5-6)
   - Added a check in the main logic to detect single-line blocks with only comments before reporting a violation (lines 63-70)
   - Implemented a new helper function `isSingleLineBlockWithOnlyComment` (lines 138-159) that:
     - Checks if the preceding element (skipping whitespace) is a left brace `{`
     - Checks if the following element (skipping whitespace) is a right brace `}`
     - Verifies the comment doesn't contain newlines
     - Returns true if all conditions are met, indicating a valid single-line block with only a comment

2. **Created comprehensive tests in `CommentWrappingRuleTestSingleLineBlock.kt`**:
   - Test for single-line block with comment: `{ /* no-op */ }` - should NOT trigger violation
   - Test for lambda parameter with comment - should NOT trigger violation
   - Test for multiple single-line blocks with comments - should NOT trigger violations
   - Test for function call with comment block - should NOT trigger violation
   - Test for multiline comment in braces - should STILL trigger violation
   - Test for block with code AND comment - should STILL trigger violation

### Key Features of the Fix

- **Minimal changes**: Only modified the `CommentWrappingRule.kt` to add the specific exception for single-line blocks
- **Maintains existing behavior**: All existing tests pass, ensuring no regressions
- **Handles edge cases**: Properly distinguishes between:
  - Valid: `{ /* comment */ }` (empty block with comment)
  - Invalid: `{ code /* comment */ }` (block with code and comment)
  - Invalid: `val foo /* comment */ = "bar"` (comment between other elements)
  - Invalid: Multi-line comments inside blocks

### Testing

- All new tests pass (6 tests in `CommentWrappingRuleTestSingleLineBlock`)
- All existing tests pass (including the original `CommentWrappingRuleTest`)
- Full test suite for `ktlint-ruleset-standard` passes with no failures

The solution correctly addresses the issue by allowing block comments inside single-line blocks (a common pattern for empty lambdas or no-op functions), while maintaining strict rules for other comment placements.

  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.BLOCK_COMMENT
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EOL_COMMENT
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.LBRACE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.RBRACE
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.hasNewLineInClosedRange
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 17→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 18→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 19→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiCommentImpl
 20→
 21→/**
 22→ * Checks external wrapping of block comments. Wrapping inside the comment is not altered. A block comment following another element on the
 23→ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 24→ */
 25→public class CommentWrappingRule :
 26→    StandardRule(
 27→        id = "comment-wrapping",
 28→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 29→            setOf(
 30→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 31→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 32→            ),
 33→    ) {
 34→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 35→        node: ASTNode,
 36→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 37→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 38→    ) {
 39→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 40→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 41→                node
 42→                    .prevLeaf()
 43→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 44→            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 45→                node
 46→                    .nextLeaf()
 47→                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 48→
 49→            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 50→                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 51→            ) {
 52→                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 53→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 54→                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 55→                    //    some comment
 56→                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 57→                    emit(
 58→                        node.startOffset,
 59→                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 60→                            "disallowed",
 61→                        false,
 62→                    )
 63→                } else if (isSingleLineBlockWithOnlyComment(
 64→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment,
 65→                        node,
 66→                        nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment,
 67→                    )
 68→                ) {
 69→                    // Allow single line blocks containing only a comment, like: { /* no-op */ }
 70→                    return
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 73→                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 74→                    emit(
 75→                        node.startOffset,
 76→                        "A block comment in between other elements on the same line is disallowed",
 77→                        false,
 78→                    )
 79→                }
 80→                return
 81→            }
 82→
 83→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment
 84→                ?.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 85→
 86→            nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment
 87→                ?.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 88→        }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun ASTNode.precedesBlockCommentOnSameLine(
 92→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
 93→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 94→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 95→    ) {
 96→        val leafAfterBlockComment = blockCommentNode.nextLeaf()
 97→        if (!blockCommentNode.textContains('\n') && leafAfterBlockComment.isLastElementOnLine()) {
 98→            emit(
 99→                startOffset,
100→                "A single line block comment after a code element on the same line must be replaced with an EOL comment",
101→                true,
102→            )
103→            if (autoCorrect) {
104→                blockCommentNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
105→            }
106→        } else {
107→            // It can not be autocorrected as it might depend on the situation and code style what is preferred.
108→            emit(
109→                blockCommentNode.startOffset,
110→                "A block comment after any other element on the same line must be separated by a new line",
111→                false,
112→            )
113→        }
114→    }
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
117→        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
118→        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
119→        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
120→        rawRemove()
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun ASTNode.followsBlockCommentOnSameLine(
124→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
125→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→    ) {
128→        emit(startOffset, "A block comment may not be followed by any other element on that same line", true)
129→        if (autoCorrect) {
130→            this.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(blockCommentNode.indent())
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it: ASTNode) = it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || !it.textContains('\n')
135→
136→    private fun ASTNode?.isLastElementOnLine() = this == null || (elementType == WHITE_SPACE && textContains('\n'))
137→
138→    private fun isSingleLineBlockWithOnlyComment(
139→        precedingLeaf: ASTNode,
140→        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
141→        followingLeaf: ASTNode,
142→    ): Boolean {
143→        // Check if this is a single-line block containing only a comment: { /* comment */ }
144→        return precedingLeaf.elementType == LBRACE &&
145→            followingLeaf.elementType == RBRACE &&
146→            !blockCommentNode.textContains('\n')
147→    }
148→}
149→
